Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

- Plan: Inspect animation frames.

end of world model.

When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The animation frames show no change in the left panel buttons (60 "w" cells in left panel throughout all frames). So the animation was purely cosmetic.
Let me reconsider the whole game. I've been trying to clear the left panel buttons but nothing works. Let me think about what I've done so far:
1. Cleared all right panel buttons (they turned from "w" to "B")
2. Tried clicking the dark square in the left panel (no effect)
3. Tried clicking the blue blob (cosmetic animation)
4. Tried clicking the yellow shape in the blue panel (cosmetic animation)
Maybe the left panel buttons are NOT supposed to be clicked. Maybe they're just part of the level design and the goal is something else.
Let me look at the bottom panels more carefully:
- Blue panel (left): has yellow shapes and white dots
- Gray panel (right): has a yellow shape and white dots
Maybe I need to click the white dots? Or maybe I need to click the yellow shape in the gray panel?
Actually, let me look at the image again. The bottom panels seem to have:
- Blue panel: 2 yellow squares and 2 white dots
- Gray panel: 1 yellow square and 2 white dots
Wait, looking at the segmentation data:
- id=110: Y at [55,56]x[20,22] - yellow shape in gray panel
- id=111: Y at [57,59]x[8,9] - yellow shape in blue panel
- id=112: W at [58,58]x[11,11] - white dot in blue panel
- id=113: W at [58,58]x[13,13] - white dot in blue panel
- id=114: W at [58,58]x[21,21] - white dot in gray panel
- id=115: W at [60,60]x[21,21] - white dot in gray panel
Let me try clicking the yellow shape in the gray panel (id=110, at [55,56]x[20,22], center ~ (55, 21)).
Actually wait, let me reconsider. The left panel has a dark square (charcoal, id=27). Maybe I need to click the dark square first, then click the left panel buttons. But I already tried clicking the dark square and it didn't work.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the left panel buttons need to be clicked in a specific order or pattern. Let me look at the pattern of the left panel buttons:
Row 33: gBBBggBBBggBBBggBBB (4 horizontal bars "_")
Row 35-37: ggwggggwggggwggggwg (4 vertical bars "I")
Row 39: gwwwggwwwggwwwggwww (4 horizontal bars "_")
Row 41-43: ggwggggwggggwggggwg (4 vertical bars "I")
Row 45: gwwwggwwwggwwwggwww (4 horizontal bars "_")
Row 47-49: ggwggggwggggwggggwg (4 vertical bars "I")
So there are 4 columns of buttons, each with alternating horizontal and vertical bars:
- Column 1: _, I, _, I, _, I
- Column 2: _, I, _, I, _, I
- Column 3: _, I, _, I, _, I
- Column 4: _, I, _, I, _, I
Wait, that's 4 columns × 6 rows = 24 buttons, but I only count 20 "w" objects. Let me check the actual layout more carefully.
Actually, looking at the ASCII:
Row 33: gBBBggBBBggBBBggBBB - these are "g" and "B" cells, not "w"
Row 35: ggwggggwggggwggggwg - "w" cells at positions 2, 7, 12, 17 (0-indexed within the slice)
Row 36: ggwggggwggggwggggwg - same
Row 37: ggwggggwggggwggggwg - same
Row 39: gwwwggwwwggwwwggwww - "w" cells at positions 1-3, 6-8, 11-13, 16-18
...
So the "w" objects are:
- Vertical bars (3 tall): rows 35-37, 41-43, 47-49 (4 columns each = 12 objects)
- Horizontal bars (3 wide): rows 39, 45 (4 rows each = 8 objects)
Total: 20 objects
Hmm, but the right panel had the same pattern and I was able to click those buttons. Let me check if the right panel buttons are still there or if they were all cleared.
Actually, looking at the current image, the right panel buttons area (below the checkerboard) shows all black "I" and "_" shapes. So the right panel was fully cleared.
Wait, let me look at the right panel more carefully. The image shows the right panel area (rows 33-49, cols 37-56) has:
- Row 33: gBBBggBBBggBBBggBBB (horizontal bars)
- Rows 35-37: gBg... (vertical bars)
- etc.
But these are all "B" (black), not "w" (light gray). So the right panel was cleared.
The left panel has "w" (light gray) buttons that I haven't been able to clear.
Let me try clicking the yellow shape in the gray panel (right bottom) to see if it activates the left panel buttons.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Left panel buttons (20 "w" objects) still unchanged. All previous clicks on bottom panels were cosmetic. Need to try the yellow shape in the gray panel.
Goal model: Try clicking the yellow shape in the gray panel (right bottom) to see if it activates left panel buttons.
Plan: Click yellow shape in gray panel at center ~ (55, 21).

</parameter>
